Title: Generation of suspension cell cultures with high syringin content and anti-inflammatory activity through overexpressing glycotransferase SiUGT72BZ2 in involucrata

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: syringin biosynthesis; monolignol glucoside branch of phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: likely glucosylates sinapyl alcohol to syringin and also promotes glucosylation of coniferyl alcohol to coniferin in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated by 50 μM MeJA for 72 h in suspension cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated by 50 μM MeJA for 72 h in suspension cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate treatment (optimal reported induction 50 μM for 72 h); suspension culture conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEarleyGate100 overexpression construct, promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative transcriptome RNA-seq of MeJA-treated vs control suspension cell cultures; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptomics; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR; Agrobacterium-mediated overexpression in S. involucrata suspension cell cultures; metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-TOF-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiUGT72BZ2 was identified from MeJA-upregulated UGTs in Group E related to Arabidopsis UGT72Es. 2. Overexpression in S. involucrata suspension cells caused a 15.2-fold increase in syringin over empty-vector control. 3. BZ2_OE also caused strong coniferin accumulation, whereas EV and CY1_OE had only trace coniferin. 4. Extracts from BZ2_OE cultures showed enhanced anti-inflammatory activity in LPS-stimulated RAW264.7 cells.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes syringin accumulation and markedly enhances coniferin accumulation in suspension cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key terminal glycosylation enzyme/control point for syringin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising engineering target to create S. involucrata cell cultures enriched in syringin and coniferin as sustainable medicinal/functional-food biomass; preferable over MeJA elicitation alone for higher product yield
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduced endogenous SiUGT72BZ2 transcript levels in BZ2_OE lines; broader effects on other secondary metabolites remain unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: recombinant protein was largely insoluble/in inclusion bodies, so in vitro enzymatic activity was not directly validated; function is supported by in vivo overexpression phenotype

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: syringin biosynthesis; monolignol glucoside branch of phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: directly glucosylates sinapyl alcohol to syringin and coniferyl alcohol to coniferin in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated by 50 μM MeJA for 72 h in suspension cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated by 50 μM MeJA for 72 h in suspension cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate treatment (optimal reported induction 50 μM for 72 h); suspension culture conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEarleyGate100 overexpression construct, promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative transcriptome RNA-seq of MeJA-treated vs control suspension cell cultures; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptomics; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR; recombinant protein enzyme assay in vitro; Agrobacterium-mediated overexpression in S. involucrata suspension cell cultures; metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-TOF-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiUGT72CY1 was identified among MeJA-induced UGTs closely related to Arabidopsis UGT72E proteins. 2. Recombinant SiUGT72CY1 directly converted sinapyl alcohol to syringin and coniferyl alcohol to coniferin in vitro. 3. Overexpression in S. involucrata suspension cells increased syringin content 5.9-fold over empty-vector control. 4. CY1_OE did not markedly elevate coniferin in vivo and did not significantly improve anti-inflammatory activity relative to EV.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes syringin accumulation, but with weaker in vivo effect than SiUGT72BZ2; minimal coniferin increase in transgenic cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a terminal glycosylation enzyme for syringin biosynthesis, but not the strongest control point in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful validated enzyme target for syringin bioengineering, though less effective than SiUGT72BZ2 for generating highly bioactive cultures
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: limited improvement in anti-inflammatory activity; transcript abundance may be under strict post-transcriptional control; little coniferin accumulation in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: authors suggest possible post-transcriptional regulation/small-RNA-like threshold control affecting CY1 expression/efficacy in suspension cells
